title: Azurefen EmbedWorks
preview:
Snapshot notes and call fragments:
- Customer profile: mid-market SaaS focused on B2B product analytics (search across docs, changelogs, support notes).
- Primary drivers: improve search relevance and surface related product docs via semantic embeddings; reduce cost per query at scale.
- Quote from CTO: 'We need sub-150ms median for question -> top-K; batch jobs can be slower but cost must be predictable.'
- Product lead emphasized: 'Nightly reindex is fine for bulk, but hot updates need <15m freshness for new release notes.'
- They began with Hosted API to iterate quickly; now assessing Dedicated because of predictable throughput and VPC controls.
- Security: SOC2 yes, SSO/SAML mandatory, KMS for key control, audit logs for query provenance, potential EU residency for a subset of customer data.
- Risk: index growth (expected to reach 12M vectors in 9 months) could blow embedding & storage spend unless we tune cardinality/quantization.
- Internal stakeholders: CTO (Aman Rao), Head of Product (Leah Morton), Infra Lead (Diego Torres).
- Notes shorthand: "POC shows promise — relevance + fewer false positives, but cost model TBD"

title: Lucent Arrow AI
preview:
Lead source: inbound, self-serve signup (trial started 2026-02-12).
Initial contact: quick email from AE pointing to quickstart; customer tried default region (us-east-1) and later eu-west-1; reported "noticeable lag for EU customers" on chat UI.
Key quote (founder): "Responses feel OK but spinner time kills engagement — we lose 30% conversion on chat flows."
Technical summary:
- Workload: mostly short-form support chat & article summarization + some embeddings for search.
- Traffic pattern: spiky around feature launches, 5-15 qps baseline, bursts to 80 qps for 30-120s.
- Latency targets: p95 < 400ms token generation latency, end-to-end chat response < 1.2s preferred.
- Cost sensitivity: medium, willing to trade small incremental cost for latency improvements.
What they tried so far:
- Default SDK settings with no explicit timeout; front-end uses 15s fetch timeout but spinner UI shows time before first token ~700-1200ms for EU users.
- Switched endpoint to eu-west-1 (hosted), little improvement; suspect routing via us-east default in CDN/edge.
- Minimal batching; each user message sent as single request, no prefix caching.
Questions they asked on call:
- Which region best for EU+NA hybrid? (we recommended route-by-region + us-west-2 for NA, eu-west-1 for EU and edge routing config)
- Recommended client-side timeout & retry settings? (we suggested 8s initial, exponential backoff, 1 retry for idempotent requests)
- Prompt-length guidance / token budgeting? (shorten system prompts, use retrieval + embeddings for long docs, advise on max_new_tokens)
Internal notes (sales/SE):
- Good PLG fit; converted from freemium but not yet paid card on file.
- Not looking at Dedicated/Private now; public cloud acceptable.
- Key persona: CTO (technical buyer) + product lead.
Recent timeline:
- 2026-02-12: self-serve signup, trial credits auto-applied.
- 2026-02-18: inbound support ticket INF-4472 about slow responses.
- 2026-02-22: 30m discovery call with AE+SE (Ava + Diego). Fireflies ff-1023-abcd.
- 2026-03-03: internal perf sampling from customer's logs (uploaded to drive link).
- 2026-03-08: follow-up email with initial recommendations; customer asked for a short POC in us-west-2.
Next steps / proposed POC:
- Provide a 1-page perf checklist: region guidance, client timeout/retry config, recommended SDK flags for streaming.
- Provide a small POC run in us-west-2 with controlled load (simulate 50 qps bursts) and measure TTF (time-to-first-token).
- Deliver recommended prompt-template edits and caching hints (prefix caching, reduce system message size).
Open blockers:
- Customer needs confirmation we can route requests from their EU users to eu-west-1 reliably (they saw CDN routing to us-east previously).
- Billing: convert trial to paid card — they prefer to test POC on card, need pricing deck.
- Dev bandwidth: small infra engineer team; need clear step-by-step guide.
Tone: informal, fragmented notes; prioritize quick wins and measurable latency KPIs.

title: Montclair KnowledgeRun
preview:
Account snapshot + recent 활동:
- Background: mid-market SaaS providing an in-app customer help center + chat. Primary goal: replace brittle keyword search with RAG + reranking to reduce deflection SLA and agent handle time.
- Scale & traffic: customer estimates ~420k canonical KB docs (moderate index size), refresh cadence daily (delta updates). Peak QPS requirement: sustained 300-450 QPS for search endpoints (support chat and widget). Burst tolerance required up to ~1k QPS for incident windows.
- Technical goals: embeddings-based retrieval (dense ANN) + lightweight cross-encoder reranker for top-5 candidates. Vector dim: 1536 (current plan), target recall at 10 ~ 0.92. Want Redwood to manage index hosting (ANN) and rerank execution path.
- Latency / cost targets: p95 end-to-end (query -> top5 rerank -> final answer) < 150ms for US region; cost sensitivity: aiming for <= $0.002 per end-user query on average, and explicit budget for reranker execution only on top-K (no full-candidate rescoring).
- Model preferences: open-weight dense encoders for embeddings, candidate rerank with small cross-encoder (quantized) or Redwood's hosted rerank variant. Must support fallback to cheaper embedding-only pipeline during capacity pressure.
- Security/compliance: Okta SSO/SAML required for admin console. SOC2 Type II required for contract. Audit logs + retention controls mandatory (retain 1 yr for audit trail). EU data residency for EU customers and snapshots. KMS integration for key management.
- PoC plan (agreed in call 2026-03-01):
  * Week 1: ingest 100k subset, validate vector encoding + search latency, configure HNSW/ann params.
  * Week 2: scale to full 420k, run sustained 2-hour load at 400 QPS, measure p50/p95/p99 for retrieval + rerank.
  * Success criteria: p95 retrieval+rerank < 150ms, recall@10 >= 0.9, average cost/query within 20% of target.
- Recent activity timeline:
  * 2026-02-05: Intro call (Ava) — product fit + pricing expectations; initial ask: demo hosted API for embeddings. (gmail thread-CAAJkxyz23)
  * 2026-02-18: Deep technical session (Diego + infra) — discussed index sizing, HNSW, vector refresh, incremental deletes. Fireflies ff_20260301_8452 referenced.
  * 2026-03-01: Executive sync (CTO + AE + SE) — CTO quote: "We need sub-150ms p95 or users will complain; cost must not blow up when reranking hits peak." Agreed POC. (ff_20260301_8452)
  * 2026-03-07: Security questionnaire kickoff; Redwood Security to provide SOC2 artifacts and KMS integration notes (fireflies ff_20260307_1921).
  * 2026-03-08: Pre-POC runbook reviewed, acceptance criteria agreed, next: POC onboarding.
- Risk notes & blockers: embedding index size ok but management of memory/replicas during high QPS is unclear; want explicit cost model for rerank per-topK. Procurement will require legal review of data residency clause.
- Helpful links / stubs: pricing deck (drive://montclair/Redwood-pricing-deck-v2), security FAQ (drive://montclair/security-faq-response), previous perf report template (drive://montclair/poc-report-template).
- Sales cues: C-suite cares about SLA for chat deflection and agent CSAT drop. Upsell path: Dedicated if traffic/cost needs favor reserved capacity; Optimize product for batching/caching recommendations could shave 30% cost.
- Next steps (short): finalize POC ingest credentials, provide sample ingestion script and index parameter recommendations, submit SOC2 artifact, schedule POC start 2026-03-15.

title: Lodestar APIWorks
preview:
- Inbound from webinar signup (self-serve). Onboarding team building a lightweight chat widget.
- Primary ask: how Hosted API handles bursts/429s — want predictable UX on onboarding spikes.
- Ben (CTO): "We can't have new signups bring the site down with 429 storms. Need short grace for initial onboarding and clear headers for client backoff."
- Concern: free-tier + onboarding email campaign will create short 2-5min traffic surges (5x normal). Need burst allowance w/ throttling not hard-block.
- Wants: per-key concurrency limits, per-route quotas, Retry-After semantics, webhook alert for quota threshold (90%), console visibility for token consumption.
- Security: SOC2 expected by audit team; SSO required for admin; audit logs mandatory.
- Demo 2026-03-02: walked through quota UI; SE showed per-route quotas + soft-burst prototype. CTO liked sample Retry-After header and incremental backoff example.
- PoC ask: 2-week trial with configurable burst token window (2 min at 5x) + telemetry on 429 rates; sample billing guard to block runaway usage >3x forecast.
- Quote: spare, choppy notes in chat — product manager asked for SDK examples for exponential backoff and client-side concurrency semaphore.
- Internal risk: limited engineering bandwidth to integrate complex retry logic; prefers Redwood to provide recommended patterns and SDK snippets.
- Next: send short doc (matrix) + SDK examples; book 60-min SE session 3/15 to prototype PoC config and telemetry events.

title: Foresail Security Labs
preview:
Account background: Foresail Security Labs (FS Labs) is the operations and R&D arm for a regional maritime port authority (critical infrastructure). They operate several on-prem data centers across NA ports. Strong interest in air-gapped private deployment for any inference workloads.

Summary of interactions: 
- 2026-01-22: Intro call (Ava) - high level requirements, primary concern: absolute network isolation + signed model binaries.
- 2026-02-12: Technical deep-dive (Diego + SE) - Fireflies id ff_2026-02-12_foresail_screening_7a9c3b. Covered: model update mechanics, kernel signing, attestations for HSM, offline package delivery. Customer: \"We need a signed artifact + repeatable attestation; no outbound calls allowed from inference hosts.\"
- 2026-02-25: Security questionnaire submitted via Drive (FORESAIL_POC_SOW_v2.pdf). They returned an initial controls matrix; asked for FedRAMP-like evidence, patch cadence, and SBOM for runtime components.
- 2026-03-02: PoC kick-off planning call. Agreed scope: offline Private bundle (Redwood Private) deployed to their lab cluster (air-gapped), sample workloads: secure_chatops (ops support), incident_response triage, reranking for policy/regulation doc search.
- 2026-03-05: On-site readiness check (SE: Diego). Hardware spec note: customer to procure NVIDIA H100 or equivalent; will allow lower-precision quantized model variants for performance.

Technical requirements & constraints (customer voice / paraphrase): 
- Must run entirely disconnected; updates via signed bundles only.
- KMS/HSM integration mandatory for key wrap; support FIPS-140 validated devices.
- Audit log retention for 7 years; ability to export immutable logs to air-gapped archive.
- Model provenance + SBOM for any runtime modules; need supply-chain attestations.
- Low-latency SLO for chat path ~ <120ms p95 for local LAN; throughput target 150 RPS for reranking batch window.
- Prefer open-models but will accept Redwood-curated quantized variants if signed and reproducible.

POC milestones / timeline: 
- Week 1 (2026-03-22): Deliver first signed Private bundle + installer; onsite install runbook review.
- Week 2: Smoke tests (connectivity proofs, no-evidence of outbound traffic).
- Week 3: Workload validation (latency/throughput bench) + KV cache persistence behavior in air-gapped environment.
- Week 4: Security review: HSM attestation, SBOM audit, patching playbook handed over.

Open technical concerns raised by SE: 
- How to perform automatic model rollbacks without outbound health-checks (we proposed local canary + signed rollback manifests).
- Need clarification on Redwood's offline update cryptographic chain and ability for customer to host signing keys inside their HSM.
- GPU driver update policy in air-gapped environments; customer requires a validated upgrade path.

Commercial notes: 
- Budget signaled by procurement: mid-six-figure ARR recurring for private instances + premium support + on-site enablement.
- Procurement timeline: internal procurement freeze until 2026-04-15, target contract signature May 2026.

Next steps (short): 
1) Deliver final Private bundle spec and offline update playbook (action: Diego; ETA 2026-03-18).
2) Legal to share NDA + tailored SOC/Sec attestation package (action: Ava; ETA 2026-03-17).
3) Schedule on-site SOC/Q&A with security engineering (action: Janelle; proposed 2026-03-22).

Quotes / snippets from customer: 
- \"If we can't attest the chain-of-trust for every model file, we can't deploy.\" 
- \"No SaaS callbacks from inference hosts — that's a hard constraint.\"

CRM shorthand / misc: 
- Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations post-POC to reduce GPU hours (caching and batching suggestions).
- Possible future: multi-site replication between port DCs with cross-site encrypted KV replication (longer-term architecture ask).

title: Hightower MedTech Analytics
preview:
Account background:
- Mid-stage medtech analytics provider; strong adoption of ML in clinical ops and triage automation.
- Heavy PHI footprint; corporate mandate: all production LLM inference must be in customer VPC or on-prem with FIPS 140-2 compatible key management.
Security/governance summary:
- Primary ask: KMS/HSM integration across multi-cloud footprint (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, GCP KMS) + BYOK capability and HSM-backed key wraps.
- Requirements called out: envelope encryption, separate crypto key per environment, key rotation policy, audit trails forwarded to customer SIEM (Splunk), and export controls for residency.
- Infosec emphasized: ability to generate and control keys (BYOK), HSM attestation (AWS CloudHSM or Azure Dedicated HSM) for signing, and granular audit logs for token-level access.
Technical notes from recent POC run (Ravi SE-led):
- POC executed against small inference cluster in customer VPC (us-east-1). Verified Redwood Private can:
  - Integrate with AWS KMS CMKs for envelope encryption of model artifacts and KV cache.
  - Accept external key wrap keys (BYOK flow) via secured upload and HSM attestation stub.
  - Export audit logs to customer S3 bucket with KMS-encrypted objects and CloudTrail events forwarded.
- Outstanding items: Azure Key Vault key import flow needs formal validation, GCP KMS IAM bindings for inference service account unclear, automated rotation handshake needs 3rd-party HSM compatibility tests.
Compliance and residency:
- Data residency: clinical telemetry must remain in US-only regions; dev/test can be global.
- Retention: audit logs must be retained for 7 years per customer's regulatory counsel (note: longer than Redwood default).
Sales/contract items:
- Interested in 12–24 month Dedicated or Private contract with optional on-prem bundle for isolated environments.
- Procurement asked for proof: (1) KMS integration runbook, (2) HSM attestation docs, (3) audit retention SLA. Drive links shared (see linked_drive_docs).
Quotes and customer voice:
- "We need to own the keys; no exceptions for production PHI workloads." — Head of IT security.
- "Latency target: p95 < 300ms for short chat/triage flows; can tolerate higher for batch summarization." — Product lead.
Recent timeline (bullets):
- 2026-02-10: initial inbound; solutions brief shared.
- 2026-02-18: discovery call (Ava + Ravi) — clarified PHI/residency constraints.
- 2026-02-25: technical POC kickoff (ff_2026_02_25_ht_strategy_call).
- 2026-03-01: POC interim review; validated AWS KMS envelope flows.
- 2026-03-03: KMS POC deep-dive with infosec (ff_2026_03_03_kms_poc_review).
- 2026-03-04: procurement provided initial security questionnaire.
Next actions (short):
- Deliver KMS integration runbook v1 (Ravi) by 2026-03-10.
- Set up Azure Key Vault import test with customer security team (target 2026-03-18).
- Prep SOC/attestation artifacts and HSM compatibility matrix for legal review.

title: CourtWise LegalTech
preview:
Lead: small legaltech startup building a secure search/summarization layer for plaintiff/defendant docs. Inbound via product signup -> support form. Wants hosted API (self-serve) if we can meet audit/logging needs and US-only residency.

Key pain: current search infra expensive, needs redaction + provenance for summaries when used in client deliverables.

Security reqs (customer quotes/highlights):
- "We need provable audit logs tied to user IDs, not just request ids."
- Must keep content in US region; SOC2 is nice but not a blocker today; retention policy controls required.

Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, low to medium ARR initially. Interested in usage tiering and developer-friendly onboarding.

Technical details discussed:
- Primary workload: embeddings + semantic search for confidential docs, and batch summarization for case briefs.
- Latency requirements: interactive search <200ms median; summarization can be async (30-60s).
- Throughput low (few dozen requests/hour early), anticipate growth if pilot performs.
- Model preference: open-models acceptable; prefer deterministic outputs and provenance.

Next actions: Ava to share hosted API quickstart + sample audit-log schema; Diego to prepare short runbook on recommended prompt/embedding settings and retention controls. Customer requested example logs and a short checklist for legal compliance.

Stated timeline: pilot in May, procurement in June if pilot OK.

Notes style: shorthand, bullets, call ids included above.

- Include quick benchmark data (latency example) for comparable model variants.

title: Clinicentrix Health Search
preview:
Summary: Clinicentrix is building an enterprise-grade clinical document search used by 200+ hospital clinicians. Primary focus: large-scale embeddings + cost-aware reranking pipeline for PHI-protected EHR notes.

Recent activity:
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Ava) — high-level product fit; they indexed a 30M note prototype.
- 2026-01-22: Deep-dive with SE (Mateo) — architecture constraints, ingestion cadence, delta indexing. Fireflies: ff:call_987654321.
- 2026-02-18: Security sync with InfoSec (Rita joined) — SSO, KMS, audit retention discussed; asked for SOC2 evidence and network topology.
- 2026-03-03: Cost model review; asked for POC SOW and expected token/embedding pricing for 120M doc index; we sent initial estimate (drive:/Sales/Deals/Clinicentrix/2026/Clinicentrix_Redwood_POC_Deck_v3.pdf).

Technical requirements / asks (customer language fragments):
- "Need P95 search latency <200ms for single-clinic queries; multi-site aggregate queries can be higher."
- Index size target: ~120M clinical notes (de-identified + PHI flagged for access controls).
- Throughput goal: sustained 150 QPS peak for search surface, burst to 500 QPS during morning rounds.
- Approach preferred: two-stage retrieval — sparse/pooled doc filter -> bi-encoder embeddings -> lightweight re-rank on top-50 -> cross-encoder rerank only for top-5 when allowed. Cost sensitivity: cross-encoder rerank on-host preferred to avoid hosted per-token costs.
- Embedding model: prefer open/traceable model; will evaluate Redwood-verified candidate models. Must measure semantic drift vs. clinical queries.
- Ingest: near real-time EHR stream, dedupe, redaction pipeline before indexing; want connectors run in VPC or on-prem agent.

Security / compliance specifics:
- PHI: must not leave customer-controlled enclave for protected fields; they want split-processing pattern (embeddings hashed in VPC, identifiable PHI stored in encrypted columns).
- Audit: full access logs shipped to their SIEM, 1y retention for audit trails + tamper-evident hashes.
- Key management: HSM/KMS integration mandatory for production.
- Data residency: US for majority, but pilot includes EU hospital (data residency question).

POC success criteria:
- Index 5M clinical notes into a private Redwood Dedicated pool in VPC with P95 <200ms at 150 QPS using bi-encoder + top-50 rerank.
- Demonstrate audit logging (immutable events exported) and KMS-encrypted keys for models.
- Cost projection for 120M index with expected 2.5M queries/month and 20% cross-encoder rerank traffic.

Open questions (sales/tech):
- Which Redwood model variants can be hosted in Dedicated with HSM-backed keys?
- Can automatic fallback be enforced to private model variant when capacity in hosted tier is low?
- Exact SLA for multi-region failover (we need an architecture doc).

Stated blockers / risks:
- Legal needs explicit PHI data flow diagram before POC kickoff.
- Networking team requires a 3-week maintenance window to enable VPC peering.
- Cost sensitivity: customer requests a discounted pilot rate and strict per-query budget cap.

Next steps (short):
- Book architecture + security workshop (internal invite: Mateo, Rita, Ava + Clinicentrix infra and InfoSec) — target week of 2026-03-15.
- Deliver POC SOW + line-item cost model by 2026-03-12 (action: Ava).
- Provide network topology template + sample data-flow diagram for PHI handling (action: Mateo).

title: Aegis Cortex Solutions
preview:
Account summary: Aegis Cortex is a finserv platform offering real-time analytics for trading desks and fraud ops. Evaluating Redwood Dedicated for low-latency inference + private networking. Heavy emphasis on throughput guarantees for sustained 1k TPS across peak windows and 99th p50/p95 tail latency targets. Procurement & legal are the gating items.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (AE Ava) - high-level product fit, AE demo, interested in Dedicated.
- 2026-02-10: Architecture deep-dive (SE Diego) - validated model types (mix of 7B/13B Llama variants + custom reranker), discussed autoscaling and reserved GPU pools.
- 2026-02-24: Capacity planning workshop - provided baseline token/sec per GPU numbers, proposed initial reservation: 8x A100-equivalent GPUs (reserved) + 2x burst.
- 2026-02-27: Workload profiling call (Fireflies ff_2026-02-27_4b9c3d) - customer sent sample logs, ran synthetic P99 tests; results: sustained throughput looks feasible but tail spikes on concurrent long contexts. Action: propose KV cache config + batching profile.
- 2026-03-03: Procurement + Security sync (Fireflies ff_2026-03-03_91f2a7) - legal provided DPA redlines, insurance broker calc, flagged SLA/penalty concerns.
- 2026-03-04: POC kickoff scheduled for 2026-03-10 (3-week POC). POC scope: validate throughput guarantees, 95/99 latency SLOs under mixed workload, autoscaling behavior, failover to hosted fallback.

POC/Technical requirements and acceptance criteria (customer quotes paraphrased):
- "We need consistent 1000 TPS with p95 < 50ms for embeddings and p99 < 200ms for chat completion under production mix."
- Throughput guarantee must include documented capacity planning and pre-warming steps.
- Prefer dedicated VPC deployment with customer-managed KMS.
- Want canary A/B rollout capability and on-demand burst for market open windows.
- Require audit logs retention 7 years for compliance team.

Procurement/security blockers (detailed):
- DPA/MSA redlines: Aegis legal insists on narrower indemnity, higher liability cap, and explicit SLAs tied to throughput (not just availability). They want an explicit remediation ladder for missed throughput SLOs (credits + right to terminate after X misses).
- SLA language: Current Redwood SLA model centers on uptime % and credit calculations. Customer insists on throughput-based credits (tokens/sec or request/sec windows) and clearer definition of what constitutes a breach (e.g., sustained 10% below committed throughput for 30m).
- Insurance: Broker requires minimum cyber/tech E&O limits that exceed standard Redwood template; procurement asks whether Redwood can provide proof of coverage and consider passing higher limits to insurance rider rather than contract amendment.
- Compliance add-ons: Need ISO27001 certificate and SOC2 Type II evidence; also data residency clauses for EU trade desks. These may trigger Private deployment add-on or specific routing rules and impact SLA (cross-region failover limitations).
- Contractual terms: Term length, termination for convenience penalties, and renewal pricing indexation need negotiation. Finance pushing for 12-month firm commit with quarterly usage reconciliation.
- Security posture: Customer requires pen-test reports and third-party vulnerability scans prior to go-live; wants remediation SLAs and a mechanism to escrow model weights if requested.

Sales strategy notes / playbook items:
- Align SE-led POC to prove throughput SLOs with real workloads early; measure both average and sustained windows (1m, 5m, 30m).
- Provide a dedicated SLA appendix draft that maps committed GPUs -> throughput targets -> credit ladder. Include pre-warm/scale-up timelines and acceptable blackout windows for maintenance.
- Loop legal early: propose a two-track negotiation - technical SLA appendix to sit alongside a narrower MSA/DPA to minimize redlines. Offer sample carrier insurance certificates and suggest using Redwood's recommended carrier + rider if needed.
- Offer a Private deployment pricing add-on to satisfy data residency and KMS requirements; clarify how this impacts escalation and SLA (e.g., single-region SLOs).

Next steps (action items):
- 2026-03-05: AE to upload draft SLA appendix to drive (drive:/deals/aegis-cortex/sla-appendix-draft-2026-03-05.docx).
- 2026-03-09: SE Diego to provision POC infra and run baseline synthetic throughput tests; share results by 2026-03-12.
- 2026-03-12: Legal to receive Redwood MSA + DPA redlines; internal legal will follow up with counter if needed.
- 2026-03-15: SLA negotiation call w/ procurement + legal + SE + AE (aim to resolve top-3 blockers).

Stitch notes / shorthand saved in CRM: procurement heavy, legal dance expected, technical feasibility confirmed but tail-latency mitigations necessary. If legal/insurance asks are within acceptable band we can close by 2026-05. Otherwise could slip to Q3.

Quoted lines from procurement: "we're fine paying for reserved capacity but need hard guarantees or an exit path if throughput claims are unmet".

Jira escalation: AI-3219 opened for POC infra flakiness (preliminary), OPS-7762 for quota/burst config.

Decision criteria (customer):
- Primary: documented throughput SLA mapped to reserved GPUs + measurable POC results.
- Secondary: contract liability/insurance acceptable to Aegis legal.
- Tertiary: integration with SSO/KMS and audit log retention policy.

Overall close probability: 60% (conditional on SLA appendix acceptance and manageable insurance terms).

title: BrightQuark Marketing Engine
preview:
Inbound SMB lead from content-marketing platform. Use-case: generate high volumes of short-form marketing copy (emails, headings, social) + per-campaign personalization. Primary constraints: cost sensitivity and throughput. Wants hosted_api (self-serve) — "fast time to production" and minimal ops. Quoted prospect: "We run thousands of short prompts per minute during campaigns; per-token economics matter more than raw model accuracy."

Technical requirements / asks:
- Latency/throughput: target 200-300 rps peak, p50 latency target ~200-300ms for single-pass generation; willing to accept higher tail if batching helps cost.
- Cost sensitivity: heavy interest in batching + prefix/KV caching to reduce token spend; asked about suggestions for 8-bit quantized variants or cheaper open models.
- Model preferences: open-models preferred (Llama2-like family) for cost control; okay with Redwood-curated variants if price/latency trade-offs clear.
- Routing/fallback: want simple fallback to smaller model for non-critical creative variants and higher-quality model for hero copy.
- Security/compliance: SOC2 baseline, audit logging for admin actions; retention controls not strict but need clear docs.

Sales notes / talk tracks:
- Position hosted API as lowest friction; emphasize token-level cost breakdown and Optimize suggestions for batching.
- Offer a short tutorial on prefix caching and how to reduce repeated prompt tokens across campaigns.
- Proposed POC: 100k token trial over 7 days with sample campaign traffic + guidance from SE on batching config.

Next steps / owner actions:
- Ava to send trial enablement link + test credit on 2026-03-06.
- SE (Liam) to provide recommended batch sizes and sample SDK config by 2026-03-08.
- Follow-up call scheduled 2026-03-09 to review POC metrics and pricing options.

Current blockers: pricing clarity for sustained bursts, exact latency SLOs during campaign peaks, and whether caching integrates with their CDN pipeline.

Misc: Gmail thread includes example workload CSV + sample prompts (thread-17c9d2b8). Drive deck (pricing + security) shared. Jira INF-4567 opened to track POC infra questions.

title: GuidedLaunch Labs
preview:
Context: GuidedLaunch builds an in-app guided onboarding copilot for freemium->paid conversion. Primary constraint is predictable latency during step-by-step guided flows and hard token caps to control cost.

Key quotes / call notes:
- Product: "Users must not wait >300ms for short helper responses (one-sentence suggestions). p95 matters more than average."
- Head of Product: "We need strict per-session token caps so power users don't blow the budget; show us cost vs quality trade-offs."

Technical requirements and preferences:
- Start with Hosted API for speed to POC; plan to evaluate Dedicated if latency/cost goals require reserved capacity.
- Streaming responses + function/tool calling for step flows (open url, execute client-side validation, fetch user context).
- Model preferences: open model variants ok if match latency + cost (quantized weights preferred). Willing to test Redwood-verified smaller variants.
- Monitoring: need per-route p95/p50, token spend per session, and rollback/fallback to cheaper model on overload.

POC acceptance criteria (agreed):
- p95 latency for single-turn helper responses < 300ms (measured in-region).
- token cost per onboarding session < $0.05 (target; CFO flexible).
- streaming behavior stable (no partials dropped); function calls executed within 2s end-to-end for simple actions.

Commercial signals:
- Prefers to start hosted (self-serve POC) and convert to Dedicated with 12-month reserved commit if SLOs met.
- Budget band discussed: $50k-$150k ARR target for year 1.

Open items / blockers:
- Legal ask: retention policy & data residency language for EU customers (they have EU users).
- Need in-production traffic replay for realistic bench (they will share sanitized logs).
- CFO wants a Dedicated price comparison that includes burst pricing and autoscaling behavior.

Action items assigned:
- AE (Ava): finalize POC plan & SOW; send hosted API trial config.
- SE (Diego): baseline benchmark script for p95 measurements, sample prompt set representing guided flows.
- Product (GuidedLaunch): provide sanitized session traces & top 20 prompt templates.

Short-hand CRM notes: demo looked good; streaming + function-calls were compelling. High sensitivity to latency and token spend. Likely to progress to procurement if POC shows <300ms p95 and cost targets met.

title: EchoHarbor Coachly
preview:
Summary of account and requirements: EchoHarbor Coachly builds a coaching layer for mid-market contact centers — post-call QA, automated coaching highlights, and turn-to-ticket summarization. Key priorities: high fidelity transcripts, low-latency highlights for supervisor dashboards, and predictable performance during end-of-month billing spikes.

Key technical asks (from SE call 2026-02-18):
- Need sub-500ms median token latency for short agent prompts (live assist) when routing to smaller context windows.
- Batch/routing policy to separate long-billable summarization jobs from live assist traffic.
- Support for tool-calling to update ticketing system (REST webhook + idempotency) and atomic action confirmations.
- Prefix caching for repeated customer intros to reduce token cost for multi-turn flows.

Security / compliance:
- Must support SSO (SAML) + SCIM; audit logs retained 12 months; KMS integration preferred for encryption at rest. Data residency: US-only for PII storage. Legal submitted SOC2 and ISO questions; security team flagged need for Private or Dedicated option.

POC plan: 2-week Dedicated run with synthetic load (peak 1.2k concurrent streams, sustained 300r/s summarization jobs). Success metrics: 99th perc latency <= 2s for live assist, summary quality F1 >= 0.78 vs internal eval set, cost per monthly active agent <= $5k under expected usage.

Sales notes / quotes:
- Customer: "We need coaching highlights surfaced within 30s of call end or supervisors won't adopt it."
- AE: focus on Optimize recommendations for batching + quantization to hit cost targets.
- SE: propose a mixed routing policy: small-model low-latency pool for live assist + quantized medium model for nightly summarization.

Open items / blockers:
- Legal DPA negotiation (redlines sent 2026-02-28).
- Security wants SOC2 Type II report and a short threat model for Dedicated deployment.
- Engineering: confirm autoscaling behaviour under synthetic spike (requested logs/benchmarks).

Artifacts and refs: pricing deck (drive link), FF transcripts for 2 technical calls (linked_fireflies above), Jira tickets for POC infra (RDW-1123), email threads with legal (linked_gmail_threads).

Next actions assigned: 
- Marcus (SE) to provision Dedicated test pool + share runbook by 2026-03-05.
- Jordan (CSM) to collect sample transcripts and coaching rubric from customer (due 2026-03-07).
- AE (Ava) to follow up on DPA negotiation and escalate to partnerships if needed.

title: Lilypad Socket
preview:
Summary: inbound SMB trial from organic signup (marketing blog link). Signed up for Hosted API; created first API key but only ~5 calls total over 10 days. Appears to be evaluating multiple vendors — CTO indicated they want 'lowest latency for chat widgets' and are attracted to competitor promo credits. Pricing confused team; misc. console errors they saw may be due to using wrong region. Wants SOC2 + SSO before committing but initial interest is self-serve Hosted API (public cloud).

Recent timeline (short):
- 2025-12-09: created account + free credits auto-provisioned.
- 2025-12-10: first API key created; 2 test completions recorded.
- 2025-12-15: automated onboarding email opened, no follow-up.
- 2026-01-20: AE outreach (intro) — got brief reply from CTO: 'evaluating latency & cost; using competitor credits for PoC'.
- 2026-02-18: 30m demo scheduled; Fireflies ff-2026-02-18-9912. Demo covered streaming & rate limits; team asked about region routing.
- 2026-02-25: trial credits showing low consumption; ticket RDW-4219 opened about perceived 429s on burst tests. Support confirmed it was client side polyfill — not platform.
- 2026-03-01: follow-up email from AE, no response. Last activity: small test request.

Use-case & requirements (from calls):
- Primary: chat widget for customer support (concurrent users 20-40 peak). Need sub-500ms p95 ideally for short queries; willing to accept higher for long-form.
- Secondary: product recommendation reranking for search results (throughput moderate, latency tolerant).
- Must be SOC2-level controls, SSO (SAML) and audit logging for compliance with vendor policy. Data residency not strict — US cloud OK.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget; looking for predictable token economics and clear burst behavior.

Observed risks / why trial is at-risk:
- Low API calls after initial key indicates blocked integration work — may be deprioritized internally.
- Competitor (OpenAI) promo credits extend their PoC runway; possibility they'll switch if no clear differentiation.
- Confusion on billing and perceived 429s created friction. No CSM assigned.

Proposed remediation (AE/SE plan):
- Offer 72h top-up credits specifically for streaming/burst tests and full support pairing session with Marcus (SE) to run a live integration (pair-program).
- Provide short playbook: 'Quick latency checklist' (region selection, client pooling, streaming sample), plus tailored cost estimate for expected query profile.
- If no response by 2026-03-12, prepare a concise one-pager comparison vs. their top competitor highlighting predictable pricing and routing fallback.

Direct quote from CTO (paraphrased): 'We like the dashboard but need to be sure we won't get hit with surprises — and the latency needs to be consistent for the chat.'

Next steps recorded in CRM: AE to send credits + availability for pairing; SE to prepare integration notes and a 30-minute lab session; target next contact 2026-03-08.

Stability/Account health: AT-RISK (trial stalled). Notes stored and will be appended with Fireflies transcript and post-pairing outcomes.

title: DevSherpa Labs
preview:
Inbound from lead form: devtool startup building a context-aware code assistant. Wants hosted API + SDKs. Key asks: support for very long conversation/context windows (project-level context spanning multiple files), predictable per-user rate limits, easy TypeScript + Python SDK, streaming completion plus chunked embeddings. Interested in prefix/KV caching to reduce token costs. Team is cost-conscious but values dev DX. 

Call 2026-02-18 w/ Founder (Sami Patel) + CTO (Leah Park) — demo run-through. Quote from Sami: "We need the assistant to keep ~200k tokens of project context and surface relevant code snippets across repos. If the model forgets state, we lose trust."

Technical notes: primary cloud = AWS (us-east-1). Want simple API key flow for early beta, SSO later. Concerned about rate limiting for CI-style batch usages (hundreds of requests/min during indexing) vs interactive editor latency. Asked about SDK tree-shaking and ESM support for web-based IDE plugin. 

Trial ask: 30-day POC, start w/ hosted_api, ingest 10 repos (private GitHub) + run up to 150k token contexts in retrieval pipeline. Wants guidance on batching/quantization via Optimize suggestions. 

Follow-ups: send TS + Py SDK snippets, sample code for streaming + reconnect, cost estimate for heavy-context sessions, link to pricing deck and security faq.

title: Midsail Helpline Analytics
preview:
Summary: midsize contact center SaaS provider focused on tier-1 chat deflection and agent augmentation. High-volume, cost-sensitive — primary goal: move 50-60% of inbound chats to self-serve + agent assist for the remainder.

Workload profile and priorities:
- Expected steady-state volume: ~120k messages/day; peak: burst windows of 10-12k messages/hour during marketing campaigns.
- Primary workloads: short chat sessions for FAQ deflection, mid-length ticket summarization (100-800 tokens), agent-assist completions, and orchestrated tool-calls for first-contact resolution.
- Latency targets: human-facing deflection responses p95 < 500ms; agent-assist p95 < 300-350ms for token streaming.
- Throughput target: sustain 200-300 concurrent real-time chat threads per region with graceful degradation under 2x spikes.
- Cost sensitivity: CFO directive to keep incremental inference spend under $400k ARR for this workload; want clear unit economics per 10k chats.

Technical requirements / preferences:
- Prefer Dedicated + VPC for isolation and predictable throughput; open to hybrid (hosted API for low-volume regions).
- Need prefix/KV caching and request batching to achieve unit-cost targets; want automatic suggestions from Redwood Optimize.
- Interested in 8-bit/4-bit quantization profiles for candidate model family to reduce GPU memory and cost. Want exploration notes from Redwood on quality tradeoffs.
- Tool-calling: must integrate with their orchestration bus (internal) — sequences of tool calls for order status lookup and policy checks.
- Security: SSO/SAML, SOC2 compliance, KMS key integration, audit logs capture for 365 days, and EU data residency for EU customers.

POC & timeline so far:
- 2025-11-24: initial intro call (Ava / Noah). high-level requirements captured.
- 2025-12-08: technical deep-dive with infra & platform (Noah + infra). asked for VPC design and network egress controls.
- 2026-01-12: pilot statement of work draft shared (drive:/decks/midsail-redwood-pilot-pricing-v1.pdf).
- 2026-02-09: fireflies ff-4891-20251118: product demo of routing/fallbacks; client liked automatic fallback to smaller models.
- 2026-02-25: hands-on POC kickoff (ff-5023-20260225). POC scope: 30-day pilot, 2 regions, 1k concurrent sessions target.
- 2026-03-01: POC day 6 interim sync: observed initial deflection 38% using baseline prompt set; latency targets met on small batch but fell to p95 ~720ms under synthetic 2x spike.
- Notes from POC run: caching reduced repeated prefix cost by ~22% for FAQ flows; naive batching introduced latency spikes for short-turn chats — need continuous batching with latency SLO enforcement.

Quotes & context from stakeholders:
- CTO (M. Reyes): "Cost per resolved chat is the metric — not tokens. If Redwood can show predictable per-ticket spend we can move to dedicated."
- Head of Support Ops: cares about visibility; wants token-level cost breakouts per queue and per-route dashboards.

Next steps (short):
- Produce detailed unit-econ model: cost per deflected chat at different deflection rates + sensitivity to batch size and quantization. Action: Redwood to provide model-run scenarios by 2026-03-12.
- Security: hand off VPC network diagram to Midsail security for review. Action: schedule security readout week of 2026-03-15.
- Pilot SOW: finalize SLA for latency p95 and fallback policy (allowed percentage of routed calls to small model).

Blockers and open questions:
- Security sign-off timeline (need SOC2 report from Redwood + architecture diagram).
- Legal: data residency addendum for EU customers and retention controls.
- Economics: CFO needs comparison vs current in-house GPU farm after factoring infra op costs.

Attachments & refs: drive:/decks/midsail-redwood-pilot-pricing-v1.pdf, ff-5023-20260225 recording, jira RD-1187 (network).

Sales notes (AE): concise and choppy — good technical fit. Call out: prioritize Optimize engagement early: suggest 3 quantization + batching profiles to evaluate in week 2 of pilot. Emphasize rollback/fallback and per-route cost dashboards.

title: Meridian Pulse Works
preview:
CRM notes:
- Account: Meridian Pulse Works (mid-market SaaS, product analytics).
- Primary contact: Riley Park (VP Product), engages with infra lead Samir J. and finance mgr Nora L.
- Use-case: customer-facing chat assistant for product docs + vector search for support workflows. Also embeddings for similarity and summarization for feature notes.
- Performance targets: target p95 latency ~100-150ms for chat; throughput ~40 rps avg, peak test saw 120 rps. Cost sensitive — finance wants predictable unit economics.
- POC summary: hosted API POC deemed functionally successful. Observed p95 tail when concurrent burst simulated; SE suspects cache warming + KV eviction under load.
- Model preference: open-family models OK (Redwood-verified profiles preferred). Must be able to pin model version and fallback to smaller variant under capacity pressure.
- Security / deployment: require VPC peering or private endpoint, SOC2 attestation, KMS integration, US-only data residency for now. SSO/SAML integration mandatory.
- Pricing asks from Meridian: 12- and 24-month commit options; monthly smoothing (commit ramp); burst pool at 2x baseline; hard overage cap or step-pricing rather than open per-token overage. Finance asked for a sample ARR schedule showing ramp Q2->Q4.
- Redwood offer at last draft: three commit bands (250k, 500k, 1M token-equivalent per month), 2x burst credits, overage $0.006/token for smallest band, sliding discount for higher commits. Proposed 3-month ramp where first month billed at 50% commit.
- Meridian feedback: likes burst model but wants explicit overage ceiling and invoice predictability. Legal pushed back on tail-latency SLA language — needs clearer measurement and remedy.
- Quotes: Riley: "We need to lock ARR visibility for the year — can’t have surprise overages."
- Internal notes: SE Diego recommends enabling prefix caching and conservative batch sizing on Dedicated; Ops suggests reserved pool w/ autoscale + pre-warm to reduce tail-latency.
- Risks: procurement timeline (license committee) could push deal to 2026-05. Technical risk: peak tail-latency without KV cache tuning.
- Wins: team liked Redwood Console traces and cost breakdown; hosted POC showed 25% cost improvement vs current infra when batching and cache enabled.
- Next step: AE to update term sheet with a tightened overage cap, explicit ramp schedule, and example invoice; schedule procurement review.

title: CodeLattice Labs
preview:
Short summary: mid-stage IDE copilots vendor building embedded code assistance for enterprise dev teams. Primary ask: low-latency streaming and deterministic UX in editors (VS Code + JetBrains). TTFT focus — want p50 <= 80ms, jitter minimized, smooth token streaming (no stuttering). Token budget policy: per-session token cap + adaptive cutoff for long completions. PoC constraints: integrate with hosted API first; must show model pinning + automatic fallback (smaller quantized model) under load.

Recent call notes (fragmented CRM style):
- "When autocomplete stutters devs lose context" — product lead quote.
- Concern: websocket disconnects cause duplicate suggestions; want idempotent deltas and resume token offsets.
- Prototype uses continuous streaming (SSE) with 16-token chunking, but jitter at client seen when GC spikes on local plugin.
- Backend asks: support pinned model per-user and per-repo; ability to route to dedicated instances for enterprise customers.
- Cost sensitivity: willing to pay more for <100ms TTFT for premium users; default users ok with 200-300ms.
- Security: must support SSO (SAML), corporate KMS, audit logs, and per-repo data residency notes.

POC details and acceptance criteria (short):
- TTFT p50 <= 80ms across 95% of sessions in EU/NA regions.
- No visible token burst > 3x after first token (jitter threshold).
- Backpressure policy ensures editor keeps first-token latency even under 100 concurrent dev sessions per org.
- Model variant fallback: if dedicated capacity saturated, auto-route to pinned quantized variant with failover TTL 30s.

Action items:
- Diego to run 1-hour stress run (50 VS Code clients) w/ pinned model and report p50/p95/p99.
- Priya to circulate security FAQ and KMS integration checklist.
- Legal to review KMS SOW.

CRM shorthand:
- next: demo w/ token-budget tuning + canary pinning (Ava)

title: Lyra Oxford Systems Ltd
preview:
Summary: Large EU-headquartered manufacturing automation vendor evaluating Redwood Private (VPC) for on-prem-like isolation. Primary drivers: data residency in EU, HSM-backed key control, demonstrable audit trails, and strict network isolation. They ran a 3-week POC (chat + document search) on Dedicated; now moving to procurement for private VPC.

Security intake / vendor risk: Legal & InfoSec requested full SIG/CAIQ deliverable. They want:
- Completed SIG questionnaire with evidence links (SOC2 cert, encryption diagrams).
- CAIQ mappings to our control implementations and a shared-responsibility table that their security team can drop into their vendor-risk system.
- Explicit network architecture: VPC peering vs. PrivateLink, NAT/egress controls, firewall rules, allowed IP ranges, and flow logs retention policy.

KMS / key management:
- Customer requires customer-managed keys (CMK) with HSM-backed KMS. Must show how Redwood integrates KMS (bring-your-own-KMS) and what keys we hold (if any).
- Need an HSM/PKI diagram and a statement of where keys are generated, rotated, and stored.
- Clarify emergency access procedures and key escrow options.

Audit logging & retention:
- Ask: retention windows (default 90 days), export formats (parquet/json), and who has access.
- They want audit logs pushed to their S3/GCS in EU region and ability to revoke Redwood console access while preserving logs.

Data residency & residency proof:
- All inference and embeddings must remain in EU region. They require attestations and a data-flow diagram showing no tokenized copy leaves region.

POC feedback (technical):
- Latency SLOs met for single-region workload (avg p95 ~120ms for chat with quantized model).
- Token costs OK but ops want Optimize suggestions to reduce tail tokens.
- Need latency comparison between dedicated pool and private VPC due to additional network hop.

Sales / commercial:
- Favoring 12–18 month committed capacity contract (Dedicated + Private overlay).
- Procurement wants license + platform fee separated; need final SOW and MSA redlines.

Quotes/paraphrases: "We need provable control of keys and logs — if you can't give us the KMS model, we can't go forward."

Immediate asks from Lyra:
1) Fill and return SIG + CAIQ spreadsheet (security team deadline 2026-03-05).
2) Provide VPC architecture diagram showing private endpoints and log export flows (network deadline 2026-03-01).
3) Draft shared-responsibility matrix (sample provided).

Internal notes: SE Diego to produce KMS/HSM diagram + sign-off Mgr. AE Ava to coordinate legal redlines with Sarah (legal). CSM Priya to prepare onboarding checklist for post-sign.

title: LegalBeacon Discovery
preview:
Summary: evaluation for enterprise RAG search across legal discovery workloads. Primary ask: embeddings + reranking pipeline with strict retention and audit logging. Corpus: estimated 25M docs (mix of scanned PDFs + extracted text), growth ~20%/yr.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2026-01-14 discovery call (FF-20260114-1532) w/ CTO (Mark Li), Head of Search (Priya Rao), AE Ava Chen. High level requirements captured.
- 2026-02-18 POC kickoff (FF-20260218-1010) — ingested 500K doc sample to test embed pipeline.
- 2026-02-25 security questionnaire submitted; security team asked for KMS/HSM diagram and retention controls.
- 2026-02-27 SE workshop w/ Diego — ran perf tests for rerank at 512-token context; saw p50 token latency ok, p99 for rerank tail ~450ms on dedicated GPU variant.

Requirements / priorities (from multiple stakeholder calls):
- Workload: near-real-time document search + refined reranking for case discovery and regulatory response.
- Scale: target production corpus 20-30M documents, peak query load ~3,000 qps (embedding-only) and ~400 qps for reranking heavy queries.
- Latency SLO: 95% <= 200ms for embedding vector fetch + similarity; rerank (single-step re-rank) target 95% <= 400ms, p99 target \"as low as possible\" but acceptable up to 750ms with fallbacks.
- Cost sensitivity: target cost-per-query (avg) <$0.01, looking to justify Dedicated vs Private based on unit economics.
- Model preferences: open/Redwood-verified models with quantization options; ability to switch to smaller reranker for cold queries.
- Routing/fallbacks: prefer smart routing by latency and cost; must support automatic fallback to lower-cost model when capacity constrained.
- Security/compliance: VPC private deployment required, customer-managed KMS (HSM integration), audit logging for all queries + operator actions, data residency in US only, retention controls configurable per-case basis (90/365/indefinite).

POC notes / technical observations:
- Vector index: currently evaluating HNSW vs. IVF-PQ for 25M docs. HNSW gives better recall but index RAM footprint high; IVF-PQ reduces memory but increases rerank latency. Diego suggested Redwood Optimize recommendations for quantization + shard sizing.
- Disk vs memory: prefer SSD-backed nodes with warm caching for common prefixes; cache hit rates measured ~18% on sample.
- Embedding cost: baseline embed generator cost ~ $0.0008/query on sample runs; reranking cost depends on model size — looking to keep rerank to 1-2 token prompts by using condensed representations where possible.
- Audit: security wants immutable audit log delivered to customer's SIEM (syslog/CloudWatch) + retention export.

Quotes / stakeholder notes:
- CTO Mark Li: \"We cannot leak eDiscovery documents — chain-of-custody + audit is non-negotiable.\"
- Head of Search Priya Rao: \"If we can get avg query below $0.01 with 95% latencies under 300ms, we can justify Dedicated+Private.\"

Next steps / open actions:
- Deliver network diagram + KMS integration plan by 2026-03-07 (Ava / Diego).
- Provide detailed cost model (embeddings + rerank per qps) and 3 sizing options (min / mid / high) by 2026-03-14.
- Security to validate audit export format; meet with security ops (requested week of 2026-03-08).

Short-term risks / blockers:
- Security needs HSM ownership contact (no contact provided yet).
- Legal wants written retention enforcement guarantees before procurement.

Related artifacts:
- pricing deck: /drive/pricing/legalbeacon-pricing-deck-v2.pdf
- security FAQ placeholder: /drive/security/legalbeacon-security-reqs.docx

Sales notes (AE):
- Warm lead; usage-driven conversion likely Q2 if security sign-off.
- Sensitive procurement window; competitor checks ongoing (Pinecone + Elastic proof-of-concept).
- Recommend accelerate Private deployment scoping and provide a one-page compliance brief for legal team.

title: Grafton Vector Labs
preview:
Summary: Grafton Vector Labs is evaluating Redwood for a RAG-driven enterprise search product used across multiple EU financial divisions. Primary concerns: strict EU/UK residency, predictable latency (<80ms target median for reranked top-5), and low cost per query (~0.4-1.2 cents targeted).

Scale & workload: planning index of ~500M small-doc vectors (250M user-facing docs + metadata), embedding dim 1536, expected sustained QPS 60-150 with spikes to 400 during market opens. Reranking: bi-stage approach (approximate ANN recall -> cross-encoder rerank). Want ability to route heavy traffic to dedicated fleets and fallback to cheaper hosted variants if capacity constrained.

Technical asks from last call: "Can you guarantee EU/UK residency for index & audit logs?" InfoSec asked for KMS/HSM integration, SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence, and 7-year audit log retention for certain pipelines. Network: requires VPC peering and dedicated egress IPs for IP allowlisting.

POC results so far (notes): initial dedicated cluster in eu-west-1 ingested 48M vectors; median query latency 95ms (ANN + cross-encoder rerank). After enabling KV/prefix caching and smaller reranker batch size, we saw median drop to ~68ms on a 3-node dedicated pool in eu-west-1. Cost per query measured in POC: ~1.4c (current config) — still above their 0.4-1.2c target. Redwood Optimize suggestions requested: quantization + batched reranker, dynamic routing to lower-cost model for low-priority queries.

Risk & blockers: legal needs explicit UK residency clause for a UK-based business unit (legal_data_residency_signoff). Latency mismatch vs internal SLOs on some long-tail queries. Need a clear multi-region failover architecture (eu-west-1 primary, eu-west-2 failover) with deterministic routing policy and warm replicas.

Customer quotes & tone: "We like Redwood's unified deployment story — but this must be provable at scale." "Cost is becoming the gating factor: if you get to 0.5c/q we can move to Dedicated contract."

Next steps agreed: 1) 7-day POC extension focused on caching + quantization tweaks (owner: Diego). 2) Provide legal residency amendment draft and SOC2/ISO artifacts (owner: Redwood Legal/Security). 3) Run multi-region failover simulation and share routing policy templates. See drive:/decks/grafton-poc-plan.pdf and ticket GRAFTON-13 for tracking escalations.

title: Mariner Spark
preview:
Inbound SMB lead from productized in-app assistant startup. Primary product: embeddable chat for customer success + in-app help; multi-tenant SaaS with 20k MAU forecast.

Key technical constraints: streaming responses w/ sub-200ms token emit preferred, p99 < 500ms end-to-end for common short-turn interactions, < 200ms cold-start desirable. Heavy emphasis on smooth UX (progressive token streaming, partial responses). Will integrate via browser SDK and server-side proxy for rate limiting.

Model preferences: open to small/medium open models with quantized variants; wants ability to route lower-cost requests to smaller models and fall back to higher-capacity models for long-running sessions. Cost sensitivity high — tight margin for token budget.

Security/compliance: SOC2 in roadmap, needs SSO (SAML) now for internal admin console, audit logging for admin events. Data residency not a blocker today (US customers) but may matter for EU expansion.

Quotes from call: "Our users drop if the assistant stutters — streaming speed is the single biggest UX gating factor." — Sara (PM).

POC ask: simple 2-week smoke test to validate streaming latency at small scale (~20 concurrent agents), sample queries set provided. Asked for sample token billing math and an estimate on 1k daily active convs.

Decision process: founder + CTO primary decision makers; procurement lightweight — credit card for startups ok. Budget: constrained; likely to convert to paid hosted plan if SLAs and token economics align.

Action items:
- Send tailored streaming-performance guide (examples + recommended SDK settings) (Ava / Diego) — due 2026-02-26.
- Provide cost estimate for 1k DAU baseline + uplift scenarios; include caching/batching suggestions.
- Offer short live lab slot for p99 measurements using Redwood hosted test endpoints (30m).

Stubs/links: Fireflies call ff_20260220_0a9b2c (transcript pending), drive pricing deck link above, Jira INF-3421 opened for follow-up perf measurement.

Notes style: shorthand, bullets, developer quotes, timeline above for quick read.

title: HarborLight Support Intelligence
preview:
Account background:
- Founded 2017; unified agent desktop for mid-size carriers and utilities. Heavy Twilio Flex + Zendesk deployment across N.A. operations.
- Peak live agents: ~4,200, projected peak growth to 6k seasonally.
- Current AI stack: small internal rules + third-party summarization for after-call notes; evaluating streaming suggestions for live assist.

Decision makers / roles:
- VP Product: Claire Monroe (primary economic buyer)
- Head of Engineering: Rajiv Mehta (tech signoff)
- Head of CX Ops: Laura Kim (SLOs / UX)
- Procurement: Daniel Ortiz

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2025-10-06: Intro discovery call (Ava + Claire) — high interest in lowering AHT and assist accuracy.
- 2025-11-12: Technical deep dive with Rajiv + Marco — demoed streaming suggestion concept; asked about p95 latency goals.
- 2025-12-03: Shared security questionnaire (HarborLight) — SSO/Okta details + SOC2 request.
- 2026-01-30: Product workshop — prototype UX flows for tool-calling (CRM writeback, ticket update). Fireflies: FF-20260130-0948.
- 2026-02-11: Commercial & deployment discussion; Redwood to propose Dedicated cluster sizing. Fireflies: FF-20260211-2015.
- 2026-02-22: Legal sent initial redlines on data residency; asked for EU-only residency option for commercial agreement.

Use-case & requirements summary (concise):
- Primary: Real-time agent assist (streaming suggestions in agent desktop) to reduce AHT and noise — suggestions must be incremental (token-by-token streaming) and appear within tight latency SLOs.
- Secondary: Automatic ticket summarization (post-call), structured fields extraction, and automatic tool-calling for Zendesk updates.
- Throughput target: support sustained 4k concurrent agents generating suggestion streams; spike handling to 6k for up to 6 hours/day.
- Latency SLO: p95 <= 120ms for suggestion token arrival; end-to-end suggestion to UI <= 150ms ideally.
- Availability/SLA: target >99.9% on business hours; graceful degradation to fallback canned responses when overloaded.
- Model preferences: prefer quantized Llama2-13B or similar on dedicated GPUs; fallback path to smaller 7B family for cost traffic.
- Output controls: guardrails required — hallucination detection, red-team filters, canned template enforcement for account numbers and PII.
- Function-calling: required for CRM writebacks and multi-step tool workflows (must support idempotent calls).
- Observability: token-level cost/latency breakdown, per-route dashboards, trace for each suggestion.
- Security: Okta SSO, audit logs retained 365+ days, KMS for key management, EU data residency for EU customers.

POC ask & plan:
- Redwood to provision Dedicated test cluster (vPC) in us-east and eu-west for residency test.
- HarborLight to provide anonymized traffic + prompt templates; request synthetic spike generator for Twilio Flex desktop.
- Success criteria:
  * sustained p95 token latency <= 150ms under 4k concurrent agents
  * correct function-calls for CRM updates 99%+ during 24h test window
  * no high-severity security gaps from questionnaire responses

Quotes / call notes (paraphrase):
- \"If we can get streaming suggestions without jitter during our morning spike, we'll pilot across 20% of seats.\" — Laura Kim
- Rajiv: \"We need explicit fallback behavior; if model latency slips, agent must still get deterministic templates.\"

Commercial / pricing notes:
- Expecting dedicated reserved commit with burst / overflow pricing. Procurement pushing for annual commit but wants EU residency clause.
- Estimated ARR range provided to procurement: $150k–$400k depending on cluster sizing and support.

Open blockers / risks (short):
- Legal: redline for data residency and audit retention.
- Engineering: last-mile Twilio Flex integration for streaming SDK (desktop injection latency).
- Cost: peak-day billing model and overage caps.

Next steps (specific):
- Redwood: deliver Dedicated sizing + formal POC plan by 2026-03-10 (Ava / Marco).
- HarborLight: share anonymized sample traffic + Okta SAML metadata by 2026-03-15.
- Schedule 24h spike run (target week of 2026-04-06).

CRM shorthand / activity log snippets:
- \"sent security FAQ (drive:/security/harborlight-redwood-sso-faq.docx) — waiting on legal Qs\"
- \"pricing deck v2 attached to thread-CA12345z\"
- Jira escalations: INF-3421 (tool-calling idempotency question), INF-3507 (latency tracing gap)

Notes for AE/SE prep:
- Bring examples of streaming token-level traces and how fallback routing/pinning works.
- Prepare quantized model perf numbers (Llama2-13B 4-bit) for p95 token latency at target concurrency.
- Be ready to show Optimize suggestions for batching/caching to reduce unit cost while preserving latency.

title: Buttercup Botlane
preview:
Inbound self-serve signup from product Hunt link. Created org and requested first API key on 2026-01-11. Issued key, onboarding email sequence sent. Very low usage after key issuance — ~5 requests/day, mainly simple hello world calls.

AE note 2026-01-20: customer says initial integration worked but they hit token budgeting confusion and swapped some traffic to competitor (OpenAI) because of promotional credits. Quote from founder: "We liked the latency but were nudged by credits and an out-of-the-box connector from our analytics tool."

SE did light debug 2026-02-03: found they were using synchronous retry wrappers that trip over rate-limits, recommended client-side backoff and smaller batch windows. SE proposed sample batching config; customer acknowledged but low priority.

Security: small team, wants SSO later. Asked for SOC2 summary and audit logging sample — docs shared (drive link). Not requesting Dedicated/Private now. Primary ask: low latency + simple pricing.

Decision factors: price and free credits from competitor, perceived model quality for short customer support prompts, ease of integration (they used a prebuilt connector elsewhere).

Current status: trial quiet. Risk: at-risk/churn — likely to fully convert only if quick win on cost or a short usage credit to run integration tests. AE attempted outreach 2026-02-22 (voicemail + email). No reply since.

Suggested playbook: extend credits, offer quick 20-min pairing session with SE to exercise routing and batching, highlight latency benchmarks vs competitor, provide code snippet for minimal client backoff and token budgeting.

Stated timeline (founder): wants to decide on a single vendor for support bot infra by end of April; will prioritize if internal usage grows past 1k queries/day.

title: HavenBridge Inference Systems
preview:
Summary: HavenBridge is a regulated fintech (quant trading + customer-facing research assistant). Primary ask: committed Dedicated capacity with explicit throughput guarantees and clear unit economics per token. They want single-tenant VPC with KMS-managed keys and 7-year audit log retention for certain datasets.

Conversation flavor: fast-moving finance customer — ‘latency is revenue’ repeated on kickoff call. High sensitivity to tail latency and predictable per-token spend for budgeting and trading P&L attribution.

Key quotes / call snippets:
- 'We need P95 below 120ms for 32-token responses and sub-500ms for 1k-token flows if possible.'
- 'If we reserve GPUs we need transparent math: how many tokens per dollar at 70% utilization.'

Commercial posture: leaning toward 12–24 month reserved commit if perf checks out. Interested in a staged commit (small tranche now + automatic scale-up option) and rights to burst to hosted pool during peaks.

Security / compliance notes: will require SOC2 Type II evidence, remote audit window, signed BAA for certain data, and contractual clause for data residency (US only).

Attachments: pricing deck (drive link), security FAQ (drive link), perf rubric (attached in Fireflies transcript).
